title: Lithocarpus sublepidotus
intro: Lithocarpus sublepidotus, also known as Taiwanese Lithocarpus, is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The health benefits of Lithocarpus sublepidotus include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithocarpus sublepidotus is commonly used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as tea, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus sublepidotus is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. This evergreen tree is native to the Himalayas and southern China. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees. Lithocarpus sublepidotus is also known as the Himalayan evergreen oak.
